title: Three equal standing pillars — the three unbreakable guarantees of Precept (prevention, inspectability, determinism) — resting on a single foundation. The .precept definition is the foundation; the three properties it makes structurally certain are the pillars. Remove any one and the structure fails. All three are essential.
conceptualBridge: The three pillars mean Precept's guarantees are structural uprights because prevention, inspectability, and determinism are not runtime policies but load-bearing columns in the definition's architecture.
decision: Removed: avg 3.625 below threshold; pillar/column motif is superseded by stronger architectural candidates.
